CineTypePage.meta_description: Explore all 16 CineType personalities, grouped into four roles. Discover how each one watches, feels about, and chooses the films they love.
CineTypePage.hero_subtitle: Every CineType is built from four dimensions of how you watch. Browse all sixteen personalities, grouped by role, and dive into the one that watches like you.
CineTypePage.aspects_subtitle: Each personality is a unique combination of where you land on these four spectrums.
CineTypePage.directory_subtitle: Grouped into four roles that share what they watch for and how they respond.
CineTypePage.cta_subtitle: Take the CineTest to discover the cinema personality that fits how you watch.

CineTypeDetailPage.meta_description: Explore the {name} cinema personality — how they watch, what they love, and the strengths and blind spots behind their taste.

CineType.role_dreamers_description: Visceral, heart-led viewers who surrender to the spectacle — cinema as wonder, feeling, and escape.
CineType.role_stylists_description: Visceral, craft-minded viewers who chase the rush and admire the machine behind it — cinema as adrenaline and craft.
CineType.role_empaths_description: Reflective, heart-led viewers who treat cinema as a mirror for the human heart — story and meaning that move you.
CineType.role_auteurs_description: Reflective, analytical viewers who treat cinema as art and argument — decoding theme, form, and intent.
CineType.aspect_gaze_description: How you watch — a private rite or a shared event.
CineType.aspect_eye_description: What pulls you in — the sensation or the meaning.
CineType.aspect_pulse_description: How you respond — surrender to feeling or read the craft.
CineType.aspect_compass_description: Where your taste travels — the familiar or the frontier.
CineType.pole_S_summary: Watch alone and absorbed — film is a one-on-one conversation with the screen.
CineType.pole_C_summary: Film is an event — best shared with people, and talked over once the credits roll.
CineType.pole_V_summary: Watch for the experience — spectacle, tension, and the goosebumps.
CineType.pole_R_summary: Watch for meaning — themes, subtext, and what a film is really about.
CineType.pole_H_summary: Let films move you — the emotional truth is the point.
CineType.pole_M_summary: Watch with a craftsperson's eye — feel through understanding the choices.
CineType.pole_A_summary: Return to the canon, the comfort rewatch, the genres you trust.
CineType.pole_F_summary: Chase the new — world cinema, the experimental, the unknown.
CineType.nostalgist_tagline: Wrapped in a blanket and a film they've seen a hundred times.
CineType.nostalgist_introduction: Nostalgists (SVHA) watch to come home. For them a film is a place to return to — warm, familiar, and safe — and the hundredth viewing of a beloved favorite means more than any premiere.
CineType.nostalgist_how_they_watch: Alone on the couch, the same worn blanket, often a film they could recite by heart. Comfort and mood matter far more than novelty, and the ritual is half the pleasure.
CineType.nostalgist_what_they_love: The classics they grew up on, feel-good staples, and the films stitched to a specific memory. They rewatch endlessly, noticing one more tiny grace note each time.
CineType.nostalgist_blind_spots: New, difficult, or demanding cinema can feel like homework. They may write a challenging film off before it has had a chance to win them over.
CineType.nostalgist_with_others: Their favorite night is sharing a treasured film with someone who's never seen it, quietly watching that person's face for the moment they fall in love too.
CineType.nostalgist_growth: Their world widens when they let one new film in for every handful of rewatches — discovering that comfort can be found in unfamiliar places.
CineType.nostalgist_conclusion: Nostalgists remind us that cinema isn't only about discovery. Sometimes the most meaningful film is the one you already know by heart.
CineType.nostalgist_strength_1_description: Devoted to the films and feelings they love, returning to them again and again.
CineType.nostalgist_strength_2_description: They let a film move them fully, without armor or irony.
CineType.nostalgist_strength_3_description: Endless rewatching makes them quietly expert in the films they treasure.
CineType.nostalgist_strength_4_description: They share their favorites generously and want you to feel what they felt.
CineType.nostalgist_strength_5_description: They know exactly what they like and rarely chase trends to fit in.
CineType.nostalgist_strength_6_description: They savor the film in front of them instead of racing to the next one.
CineType.nostalgist_weakness_1_description: They rarely venture beyond the familiar, even when the new might delight them.
CineType.nostalgist_weakness_2_description: They can over-idealize the films of the past and resist the present.
CineType.nostalgist_weakness_3_description: Demanding or slow cinema gets dismissed before it's understood.
CineType.nostalgist_weakness_4_description: Their taste can settle into a single comfortable lane.
CineType.nostalgist_weakness_5_description: Feeling sometimes overrides a clear-eyed look at a film's flaws.
CineType.nostalgist_weakness_6_description: Old opinions are slow to update, even with good reason.
CineType.daydreamer_tagline: Drifts into a new world the moment the lights go down.
CineType.daydreamer_epigraph: We are such stuff as dreams are made on.
CineType.daydreamer_introduction: Daydreamers (SVHF) watch to be transported. Quietly adventurous, they slip into strange and beautiful worlds on their own, chasing the wonder a film can conjure more than the plot it follows.
CineType.daydreamer_how_they_watch: Alone and fully immersed, lights low, ready to be swept somewhere they've never been. They'll happily gamble on a film no one has heard of if the images promise magic.
CineType.daydreamer_what_they_love: Fantasy, animation, the surreal and the dreamlike — anything with a world worth getting lost in. Atmosphere and imagination move them more than realism ever could.
CineType.daydreamer_blind_spots: Grounded, talky, or down-to-earth films can leave them restless. They sometimes prize a gorgeous world over a story that actually holds together.
CineType.daydreamer_with_others: They struggle to explain why a film moved them, but light up sharing the one image or moment that's lived in their head for years.
CineType.daydreamer_growth: They grow by letting a quiet, realistic film in now and then — finding that wonder can live in an ordinary kitchen as easily as a far-off galaxy.
CineType.daydreamer_conclusion: Daydreamers keep cinema's sense of wonder alive, reminding the rest of us that a film can still feel like a door into somewhere else.
CineType.daydreamer_strength_1_description: They meet a film halfway, filling its world with their own wonder.
CineType.daydreamer_strength_2_description: An unknown title or strange premise is an invitation, not a risk.
CineType.daydreamer_strength_3_description: Few viewers surrender to a world as completely as they do.
CineType.daydreamer_strength_4_description: They're drawn to the odd, the original, and the unclassifiable.
CineType.daydreamer_strength_5_description: They feel a film's images and atmosphere deeply.
CineType.daydreamer_strength_6_description: They watch expecting to be amazed — and often are.
CineType.daydreamer_weakness_1_description: If a film stops dazzling them, their attention drifts.
CineType.daydreamer_weakness_2_description: A beautiful world can win them over despite a weak story.
CineType.daydreamer_weakness_3_description: Grounded, everyday films can feel flat and unrewarding.
CineType.daydreamer_weakness_4_description: They feel a film intensely but struggle to say why.
CineType.daydreamer_weakness_5_description: They sometimes use films to avoid rather than to engage.
CineType.daydreamer_weakness_6_description: Their wandering taste can leave their watchlist a beautiful mess.
CineType.superfan_tagline: Lives for opening night, with their people, in their seats.
CineType.superfan_epigraph: I love the smell of napalm in the morning.
CineType.superfan_introduction: Superfans (CVHA) are cinema's beating heart. Loyal, loud, and gloriously enthusiastic, they live inside the worlds and franchises they love and want nothing more than to share that joy with a crowd.
CineType.superfan_how_they_watch: Opening night, best seats, friends in tow, often in costume. They rewatch their favorites until they know every beat, then cheer the beats they know are coming.
CineType.superfan_what_they_love: Big franchises, beloved sagas, the films and characters they've grown up alongside. Spectacle plus belonging is their sweet spot — fandom is a home, not a hobby.
CineType.superfan_blind_spots: Devotion can curdle into defensiveness; criticism of a beloved film can feel personal. The unfamiliar and the un-hyped can slip past them entirely.
CineType.superfan_with_others: They're the engine of every watch party — organizing, hyping, quoting, and pulling newcomers into the fold with pure contagious joy.
CineType.superfan_growth: They grow by stepping outside the franchise now and then, learning that the thrill of belonging can be found in small, strange films too.
CineType.superfan_conclusion: Superfans keep the communal magic of cinema alive — the gasp, the cheer, the shared roar of a crowd that loves the same thing you do.
CineType.superfan_strength_1_description: Their excitement is contagious and turns any screening into an event.
CineType.superfan_strength_2_description: They stand by the films and franchises they love through thick and thin.
CineType.superfan_strength_3_description: They gather people and make watching a shared celebration.
CineType.superfan_strength_4_description: Within their fandom, their recall of lore and detail is encyclopedic.
CineType.superfan_strength_5_description: They pull newcomers in and want everyone to feel the joy they feel.
CineType.superfan_strength_6_description: They cheer, gasp, and live every beat out loud.
CineType.superfan_weakness_1_description: Criticism of a beloved film can feel like a personal attack.
CineType.superfan_weakness_2_description: Anticipation can outrun judgment about whether a film is good.
CineType.superfan_weakness_3_description: Loyalty to one fandom can shut out everything outside it.
CineType.superfan_weakness_4_description: They can keep returning to the same handful of worlds.
CineType.superfan_weakness_5_description: Sky-high anticipation sometimes sets a film up to disappoint.
CineType.superfan_weakness_6_description: What's new and loud can crowd out the quiet and worthwhile.
CineType.thrillseeker_tagline: First in line for whatever everyone will be talking about.
CineType.thrillseeker_introduction: Thrillseekers (CVHF) chase the rush. Energetic, social, and always hungry for the next big thing, they want cinema loud, immediate, and shared — the bigger the screen and the crowd, the better.
CineType.thrillseeker_how_they_watch: On the biggest screen they can find, with friends, on opening weekend. They'll try anything once if it promises a jolt, and they'd rather feel too much than too little.
CineType.thrillseeker_what_they_love: Event movies, action, horror, anything that grabs them by the collar. Novelty and adrenaline drive them; a film that plays it safe bores them fast.
CineType.thrillseeker_blind_spots: Slow, quiet, or reflective films lose them. In chasing intensity they can mistake the loudest film for the best one.
CineType.thrillseeker_with_others: They're the friend dragging the group to the midnight premiere and texting everyone the second the credits roll — they need to share the high.
CineType.thrillseeker_growth: They grow by sitting with a slow film and letting tension build instead of explode — discovering a quieter, deeper kind of thrill.
CineType.thrillseeker_conclusion: Thrillseekers keep cinema exciting, reminding us that a film can still grab a whole room by the throat and refuse to let go.
CineType.thrillseeker_strength_1_description: They'll try anything new if it promises a jolt of energy.
CineType.thrillseeker_strength_2_description: Their appetite for the big and bold makes every outing an event.
CineType.thrillseeker_strength_3_description: They turn watching into a shared adrenaline rush.
CineType.thrillseeker_strength_4_description: They'll say yes to a last-minute screening without a second thought.
CineType.thrillseeker_strength_5_description: They always know what's about to be everywhere.
CineType.thrillseeker_strength_6_description: Intensity, shock, and spectacle excite rather than scare them off.
CineType.thrillseeker_weakness_1_description: Slow or quiet films lose them within minutes.
CineType.thrillseeker_weakness_2_description: They can mistake the loudest film for the best one.
CineType.thrillseeker_weakness_3_description: Once the novelty fades, so does their interest.
CineType.thrillseeker_weakness_4_description: They feel a film's punch but skip its deeper meaning.
CineType.thrillseeker_weakness_5_description: The fear of missing the moment can steer their whole watchlist.
CineType.thrillseeker_weakness_6_description: A diet of pure intensity can dull their taste for nuance.
CineType.connoisseur_tagline: Rewatches to study the seams, not just the story.
CineType.connoisseur_epigraph: The whole point is to make the audience feel it without seeing how.
CineType.connoisseur_introduction: Connoisseurs (SVMA) love the craft of spectacle. Solitary and exacting, they return to the genre films they admire to study how they work — the cut, the stunt, the lighting, the perfect needle drop.
CineType.connoisseur_how_they_watch: Alone and attentive, often rewinding a sequence to see how it was built. They prize technique and revisit their favorites the way others revisit a great album.
CineType.connoisseur_what_they_love: The masters of genre and style — action, thrillers, horror, noir done immaculately. They savor a flawless set-piece more than a clever theme.
CineType.connoisseur_blind_spots: Chasing technical perfection, they can undervalue a rough film with a big heart, or dismiss popular hits as beneath them.
CineType.connoisseur_with_others: They're a quiet fountain of insight, happy to explain exactly why a scene works — if you ask, and if you're ready to listen.
CineType.connoisseur_growth: They grow by letting a messy, emotional, imperfect film land — learning that not every great moment is built on technique.
CineType.connoisseur_conclusion: Connoisseurs guard the craft of cinema, reminding us that spectacle, done right, is its own kind of art.
CineType.connoisseur_strength_1_description: They notice craft and quality others miss entirely.
CineType.connoisseur_strength_2_description: Deep, repeated study makes them genuine experts in their genres.
CineType.connoisseur_strength_3_description: A single perfectly built shot can make their night.
CineType.connoisseur_strength_4_description: Their taste is their own, immune to hype and consensus.
CineType.connoisseur_strength_5_description: They'll rewatch and rewind to understand exactly how a film works.
CineType.connoisseur_strength_6_description: They hold films to a high bar and can tell you precisely why.
CineType.connoisseur_weakness_1_description: They can look down on crowd-pleasers and popular taste.
CineType.connoisseur_weakness_2_description: Focus on craft can crowd out a film's emotional pull.
CineType.connoisseur_weakness_3_description: A small technical flaw can sour a whole film for them.
CineType.connoisseur_weakness_4_description: Their deep lane can keep them from broadening out.
CineType.connoisseur_weakness_5_description: Few films clear the bar they've set.
CineType.connoisseur_weakness_6_description: They share insight only when invited, and can seem standoffish.
CineType.prospector_tagline: Always three clicks deep into something obscure and brilliant.
CineType.prospector_introduction: Prospectors (SVMF) dig for gold. Solitary and relentlessly curious, they hunt down the cult classic, the forgotten gem, and the gloriously strange — and they love understanding why a film works as much as watching it.
CineType.prospector_how_they_watch: Alone, deep in a rabbit hole, three films into a director's filmography no one else has heard of. The thrill of the find is half the fun.
CineType.prospector_what_they_love: Cult cinema, B-movies, the obscure and the overlooked — the weirder and more original, the better. They prize discovery and the craft hiding in unlikely places.
CineType.prospector_blind_spots: Chasing the obscure, they can dismiss the mainstream on principle and value novelty over quality. Sometimes the famous film is famous for a reason.
CineType.prospector_with_others: They're a bottomless well of recommendations you've never heard of — and live for the moment a hidden gem they championed finally clicks for someone else.
CineType.prospector_growth: They grow by revisiting a beloved blockbuster with fresh eyes — finding that popular and brilliant aren't opposites after all.
CineType.prospector_conclusion: Prospectors keep cinema's margins alive, dragging forgotten treasures back into the light for the rest of us to discover.
CineType.prospector_strength_1_description: No corner of cinema is too obscure for them to explore.
CineType.prospector_strength_2_description: They form their own taste far from the mainstream.
CineType.prospector_strength_3_description: They can track down a film almost no one else can find.
CineType.prospector_strength_4_description: They spot the craft and originality hiding in overlooked films.
CineType.prospector_strength_5_description: The stranger and more unknown a film, the more it draws them.
CineType.prospector_strength_6_description: They love putting a hidden gem in someone else's hands.
CineType.prospector_weakness_1_description: They can reject the popular simply for being popular.
CineType.prospector_weakness_2_description: Newer and weirder isn't always better, but it tempts them.
CineType.prospector_weakness_3_description: They can wear obscure taste like a badge of superiority.
CineType.prospector_weakness_4_description: Endless digging leaves their viewing sprawling and unfocused.
CineType.prospector_weakness_5_description: They sometimes write off the mainstream without a real look.
CineType.prospector_weakness_6_description: Once a film is widely known, it loses some of its shine for them.
CineType.aficionado_tagline: Runs the watch party and the post-credits debate.
CineType.aficionado_epigraph: Say hello to my little friend.
CineType.aficionado_introduction: Aficionados (CVMA) are the host with the genre encyclopedia. Social and deeply knowledgeable, they love gathering people around the films they're expert in — and the lively debate that follows.
CineType.aficionado_how_they_watch: Watch party at their place, snacks dialed in, the perfect double feature programmed. They know the trivia, the making-of, and exactly when to pause for a story.
CineType.aficionado_what_they_love: The genres they've mastered — action, horror, the cult canon — shared with a room. Craft plus company is the dream; a great film is better with an audience to dissect it.
CineType.aficionado_blind_spots: They can dominate the conversation and steer every night toward their wheelhouse, leaving quieter tastes unheard.
CineType.aficionado_with_others: They're the friend everyone asks for a recommendation, and the one who turns a casual movie night into an event with a theme and a takeaway.
CineType.aficionado_growth: They grow by handing someone else the remote — discovering films outside their expertise and the joy of being the student again.
CineType.aficionado_conclusion: Aficionados turn watching into a shared craft, keeping the communal pleasure of really knowing a genre alive.
CineType.aficionado_strength_1_description: Their command of the genres they love is encyclopedic.
CineType.aficionado_strength_2_description: They make watching a warm, lively group event.
CineType.aficionado_strength_3_description: They program the night and pour the expertise freely.
CineType.aficionado_strength_4_description: They can explain exactly why a film works, on the spot.
CineType.aficionado_strength_5_description: Their passion pulls everyone deeper into the film.
CineType.aficionado_strength_6_description: Ask for a recommendation and they'll nail it every time.
CineType.aficionado_weakness_1_description: They can take over the night and the conversation.
CineType.aficionado_weakness_2_description: They steer most picks back toward their comfort genres.
CineType.aficionado_weakness_3_description: Sharing expertise can tip into lecturing.
CineType.aficionado_weakness_4_description: They like the night run their way, with their rules.
CineType.aficionado_weakness_5_description: Outside their wheelhouse, their curiosity dims.
CineType.aficionado_weakness_6_description: Strong opinions can drown out quieter ones in the room.
CineType.tastemaker_tagline: Saw it before it was cool — and made sure you did too.
CineType.tastemaker_epigraph: I'm gonna make him an offer he can't refuse.
CineType.tastemaker_introduction: Tastemakers (CVMF) are cinema's scouts. Social, sharp, and always ahead of the curve, they sniff out the next big thing and can't rest until they've put it in everyone's hands.
CineType.tastemaker_how_they_watch: Early, often, and out loud — at festivals, premieres, and group watches, with a critic's eye and a evangelist's energy. Being first matters as much as being right.
CineType.tastemaker_what_they_love: The fresh, the buzzy, the just-breaking — bold genre work and rising directors. They love spotting craft on the way up and calling it before the crowd does.
CineType.tastemaker_blind_spots: Chasing the cutting edge, they can overhype the merely new and lose patience with the old. Sometimes the hot film cools fast.
CineType.tastemaker_with_others: They're the friend whose recommendations you trust precisely because they got there first — the personal algorithm with great taste.
CineType.tastemaker_growth: They grow by sitting with a film long enough to judge it on its merits, not its momentum — and revisiting yesterday's hype with honest eyes.
CineType.tastemaker_conclusion: Tastemakers keep cinema moving, surfacing tomorrow's classics today and pulling the rest of us along for the ride.
CineType.tastemaker_strength_1_description: They find the next big thing before anyone else.
CineType.tastemaker_strength_2_description: People genuinely change what they watch on their say-so.
CineType.tastemaker_strength_3_description: They spot rising talent and craft early and accurately.
CineType.tastemaker_strength_4_description: They love sharing a find and watching it catch on.
CineType.tastemaker_strength_5_description: The new and unproven excites rather than worries them.
CineType.tastemaker_strength_6_description: They can sell you on a film in a single sentence.
CineType.tastemaker_weakness_1_description: They can overrate a film for being new and buzzy.
CineType.tastemaker_weakness_2_description: Older or slower-burning films struggle to hold them.
CineType.tastemaker_weakness_3_description: Yesterday's discovery is quickly abandoned for today's.
CineType.tastemaker_weakness_4_description: Being first can matter more than being right.
CineType.tastemaker_weakness_5_description: Their enthusiasm can fade as fast as it flares.
CineType.tastemaker_weakness_6_description: They rarely sit with a film once the moment has passed.
CineType.romantic_tagline: Keeps a few films close, like old letters.
CineType.romantic_introduction: Romantics (SRHA) hold cinema close to the chest. Quiet and tender-hearted, they return to the handful of films that have shaped them, reading their own life in the stories that moved them most.
CineType.romantic_how_they_watch: Alone, unhurried, often revisiting the dramas and love stories that mean the most. They watch for the ache and the tenderness, and they're in no rush to add to the list.
CineType.romantic_what_they_love: Character-driven dramas, aching romances, coming-of-age stories — films about people and feeling. Meaning matters, but it's the emotion they keep.
CineType.romantic_blind_spots: They can stay inside a small emotional world, returning to the same heartbreaks and resisting films that don't promise to move them.
CineType.romantic_with_others: They rarely push their films on anyone, but sharing a beloved one with the right person feels like handing over a piece of themselves.
CineType.romantic_growth: They grow by widening the circle of stories they let in — discovering new heartbreaks and tendernesses beyond their cherished few.
CineType.romantic_conclusion: Romantics remind us that some films aren't watched so much as kept — held close and returned to like a memory you don't want to lose.
CineType.romantic_strength_1_description: They feel a film's emotional truth deeply and sincerely.
CineType.romantic_strength_2_description: They return to the films that shaped them with quiet devotion.
CineType.romantic_strength_3_description: They read their own life in the stories that move them.
CineType.romantic_strength_4_description: They sit with a film's feeling long after it ends.
CineType.romantic_strength_5_description: They watch without irony, open to being moved.
CineType.romantic_strength_6_description: They know exactly which stories speak to them and why.
CineType.romantic_weakness_1_description: Feeling can override a clear look at a film's flaws.
CineType.romantic_weakness_2_description: They can stay within a small, familiar emotional world.
CineType.romantic_weakness_3_description: Films outside their comfort zone get little patience.
CineType.romantic_weakness_4_description: They gravitate to ache and can overlook lighter joys.
CineType.romantic_weakness_5_description: They rarely share their films, even when others would treasure them.
CineType.romantic_weakness_6_description: The films of their past can outshine anything new.
CineType.wanderer_tagline: Travels the world one moving film at a time.
CineType.wanderer_epigraph: The world is a book, and those who do not travel read only a page.
CineType.wanderer_introduction: Wanderers (SRHF) seek the human story everywhere. Solitary and endlessly curious, they roam world cinema in search of films that move them — different languages, lives, and ways of feeling.
CineType.wanderer_how_they_watch: Alone, subtitles on, open to a film from anywhere. They follow their heart across borders and eras, collecting the small, true moments that connect us all.
CineType.wanderer_what_they_love: International drama, intimate festival films, stories of ordinary lives lived far from their own. They chase empathy more than escape — the deeper and more human, the better.
CineType.wanderer_blind_spots: In always reaching for the new and far-flung, they can overlook the gems on their own doorstep, or wear their adventurous taste a little proudly.
CineType.wanderer_with_others: They love opening someone's eyes to a film from a place they'd never have looked — a quiet ambassador for the wider world of cinema.
CineType.wanderer_growth: They grow by sitting still with the familiar now and then — finding that a story from home can move them as much as one from across the globe.
CineType.wanderer_conclusion: Wanderers keep cinema's borders open, reminding us that the most moving story might be one in a language we don't yet speak.
CineType.wanderer_strength_1_description: No country, era, or language is off their map.
CineType.wanderer_strength_2_description: They connect with lives and feelings far from their own.
CineType.wanderer_strength_3_description: They're forever reaching for a story they haven't met yet.
CineType.wanderer_strength_4_description: Subtitles and unfamiliar names are an invitation, not a barrier.
CineType.wanderer_strength_5_description: They sit with what a film reveals about being human.
CineType.wanderer_strength_6_description: Their taste follows their heart, not the charts.
CineType.wanderer_weakness_1_description: The familiar can feel too small to hold their attention.
CineType.wanderer_weakness_2_description: They can miss the gems closest to them.
CineType.wanderer_weakness_3_description: Their pursuit of meaning can leave little room for fun.
CineType.wanderer_weakness_4_description: Roaming so widely keeps their viewing unanchored.
CineType.wanderer_weakness_5_description: Adventurous taste can shade into a sense of superiority.
CineType.wanderer_weakness_6_description: Their wandering makes their taste tricky to share.
CineType.companion_tagline: Wants you to feel exactly what they felt.
CineType.companion_introduction: Companions (CRHA) watch to connect. Warm and generous, they treasure the films that move them most when shared — and there's no greater joy than seeing a loved one feel exactly what they felt.
CineType.companion_how_they_watch: Side by side on the couch, with the people they love, often returning to the meaningful favorites they want to pass on. The company is the point as much as the film.
CineType.companion_what_they_love: Heartfelt dramas, gentle love stories, films that bring people together. Emotion shared beats emotion alone, and they curate for the heart of whoever's beside them.
CineType.companion_blind_spots: They can avoid films that might unsettle the mood, and lean so hard on shared favorites that they rarely strike out into the unknown.
CineType.companion_with_others: They're the one who plans the perfect movie night for someone else — choosing a film like choosing a gift, just to watch it land.
CineType.companion_growth: They grow by watching something challenging on their own — learning that not every film has to be shared to be worth seeing.
CineType.companion_conclusion: Companions remind us that the films we love best are often the ones we watched with someone we love — that cinema, at heart, is togetherness.
CineType.companion_strength_1_description: They make every shared watch feel like an act of care.
CineType.companion_strength_2_description: They choose films for others like they're choosing gifts.
CineType.companion_strength_3_description: They read the room and pick exactly the right film for it.
CineType.companion_strength_4_description: They use cinema to bring people closer together.
CineType.companion_strength_5_description: They watch openly, eager to feel and to share the feeling.
CineType.companion_strength_6_description: They remember what moves the people they love.
CineType.companion_weakness_1_description: They avoid films that might sour a shared mood.
CineType.companion_weakness_2_description: Watching alone can feel a little hollow to them.
CineType.companion_weakness_3_description: They lean on shared favorites over risky new ground.
CineType.companion_weakness_4_description: They can bury their own taste to suit the room.
CineType.companion_weakness_5_description: Feeling can outweigh a film's actual quality.
CineType.companion_weakness_6_description: Their picks circle the same comforting handful of films.
CineType.evangelist_tagline: Won't rest until you've seen the film that changed them.
CineType.evangelist_epigraph: Sometimes it is the people no one imagines anything of who do the things no one can imagine.
CineType.evangelist_introduction: Evangelists (CRHF) carry a torch for the films that matter. Passionate and outspoken, they champion the moving, the overlooked, and the important — and they want the whole world to feel what they felt.
CineType.evangelist_how_they_watch: Out in the world and online — at festivals, in discussions, in the group chat — always with a film they're certain you need to see. Sharing is inseparable from watching.
CineType.evangelist_what_they_love: Bold, humane, socially alive cinema, especially the underseen and the underdog. New voices and untold stories light them up; they watch to be changed and to change minds.
CineType.evangelist_blind_spots: Their fervor can tip into preachiness, and judging films by their message can blur whether the film itself is any good.
CineType.evangelist_with_others: They're the friend who won't stop recommending the film that changed their life — and who builds whole communities around the stories they believe in.
CineType.evangelist_growth: They grow by letting a film simply entertain them — discovering that not everything has to teach or transform to be worth the time.
CineType.evangelist_conclusion: Evangelists keep cinema's conscience awake, making sure the films that should be seen actually are.
CineType.evangelist_strength_1_description: They believe in films fiercely and share that belief loudly.
CineType.evangelist_strength_2_description: They can talk almost anyone into giving a film a chance.
CineType.evangelist_strength_3_description: They champion stories they think the world needs to see.
CineType.evangelist_strength_4_description: They gather people around the films that matter to them.
CineType.evangelist_strength_5_description: New voices and untold stories move them deeply.
CineType.evangelist_strength_6_description: They give their time to put important films in front of others.
CineType.evangelist_weakness_1_description: Their fervor can tip into lecturing.
CineType.evangelist_weakness_2_description: They can prize what a film says over how good it is.
CineType.evangelist_weakness_3_description: A recommendation can feel more like a homework assignment.
CineType.evangelist_weakness_4_description: They can struggle to just relax and enjoy a film.
CineType.evangelist_weakness_5_description: They can look down on films they see as shallow.
CineType.evangelist_weakness_6_description: Always onto the next cause, they rarely sit still with a film.
CineType.archivist_tagline: Has read more about the film than most have watched.
CineType.archivist_epigraph: Cinema is a matter of what's in the frame and what's out.
CineType.archivist_introduction: Archivists (SRMA) are keepers of the canon. Solitary and scholarly, they work patiently through film history, studying the masters and preserving the context that gives great cinema its weight.
CineType.archivist_how_they_watch: Alone and attentive, often with the commentary on and a stack of reading nearby. They revisit the classics to understand them more deeply, not just to enjoy them again.
CineType.archivist_what_they_love: The established canon — the masterpieces, the movements, the directors history has anointed. They love the context as much as the film: who made it, when, and why it matters.
CineType.archivist_blind_spots: Reverence for the canon can make them dismissive of the new and the popular, and the scholarship can crowd out simple pleasure.
CineType.archivist_with_others: They're a deep well of film history, happy to trace a lineage from a modern shot back to its silent-era root — for anyone curious enough to ask.
CineType.archivist_growth: They grow by watching something brand-new with no scholarship to lean on — meeting a film on its own terms, the way audiences once met the classics.
CineType.archivist_conclusion: Archivists keep cinema's memory intact, making sure the films that built the art form are never forgotten.
CineType.archivist_strength_1_description: Their knowledge of film history runs deep and wide.
CineType.archivist_strength_2_description: They'll study a film and its context for as long as it takes.
CineType.archivist_strength_3_description: They understand why a canonical film earned its place.
CineType.archivist_strength_4_description: They watch the film, then the commentary, then the criticism.
CineType.archivist_strength_5_description: They see every film as part of a larger lineage.
CineType.archivist_strength_6_description: They treat the canon with genuine, careful respect.
CineType.archivist_weakness_1_description: They can undervalue anything made recently.
CineType.archivist_weakness_2_description: Respect for the canon can harden into rigidity.
CineType.archivist_weakness_3_description: Scholarship can crowd out the simple joy of watching.
CineType.archivist_weakness_4_description: They can imply a film doesn't count without the right pedigree.
CineType.archivist_weakness_5_description: New movements have to prove themselves before they'll look.
CineType.archivist_weakness_6_description: Studying a film can keep them from feeling it.
CineType.theorist_tagline: Three subtitled masterpieces deep before breakfast.
CineType.theorist_epigraph: A film is never really good unless the camera is an eye in the head of a poet.
CineType.theorist_introduction: Theorists (SRMF) live on cinema's frontier of ideas. Solitary and fiercely analytical, they seek out the experimental, the demanding, and the unclassifiable — films to think with, the harder the better.
CineType.theorist_how_they_watch: Alone and undistracted, often pausing to turn an idea over, drawn to the films most viewers would call difficult. Ambiguity isn't a flaw to them — it's the invitation.
CineType.theorist_what_they_love: Avant-garde and arthouse cinema, slow films, formal experiments, anything that breaks the rules with purpose. They prize originality of thought above all.
CineType.theorist_blind_spots: In chasing the cerebral, they can mistake difficulty for depth and lose touch with the simple, emotional power of a straightforward film.
CineType.theorist_with_others: They're a dazzling, occasionally exhausting conversation — full of theory and connection, best met by someone who likes to argue ideas.
CineType.theorist_growth: They grow by letting a film make them feel before it makes them think — remembering that not every great film is a puzzle to solve.
CineType.theorist_conclusion: Theorists push cinema forward, championing the strange and rule-breaking films that expand what the art form can do.
CineType.theorist_strength_1_description: They read a film's form and ideas with rare precision.
CineType.theorist_strength_2_description: Demanding, ambiguous films energize rather than deter them.
CineType.theorist_strength_3_description: They seek out what no one else is watching or thinking.
CineType.theorist_strength_4_description: Consensus and acclaim hold no sway over their taste.
CineType.theorist_strength_5_description: They'll engage with anything, however challenging.
CineType.theorist_strength_6_description: Every film is a new idea to take apart and examine.
CineType.theorist_weakness_1_description: They can prize difficulty for its own sake.
CineType.theorist_weakness_2_description: Analysis can crowd out a film's emotional pull.
CineType.theorist_weakness_3_description: Their taste can feel impenetrable to everyone else.
CineType.theorist_weakness_4_description: They can reject a film simply for being widely loved.
CineType.theorist_weakness_5_description: They can analyze the life out of a simple pleasure.
CineType.theorist_weakness_6_description: Their frontier wandering leaves few people able to follow.
CineType.critic_tagline: The friend whose star ratings you actually trust.
CineType.critic_epigraph: It's not what a movie is about, it's how it is about it.
CineType.critic_introduction: Critics (CRMA) are the trusted voice in the room. Sociable and sharp, they watch the canon with a discerning eye and love nothing more than a good argument about what makes a film work.
CineType.critic_how_they_watch: Engaged and evaluative, already drafting the review in their head, then eager to test it against everyone else's. Watching and discussing are two halves of the same act.
CineType.critic_what_they_love: The respected canon and the serious new release alike — films worth taking seriously and debating. They prize a well-made, meaningful film and the conversation it sparks.
CineType.critic_blind_spots: The reflex to judge can get in the way of simply enjoying, and confidence in their verdict can shade into stubbornness.
CineType.critic_with_others: They're the one everyone asks 'was it any good?' — and the one who'll happily defend a four-star rating against all comers.
CineType.critic_growth: They grow by letting a film wash over them before they rate it — and by staying open to changing a verdict they've already filed.
CineType.critic_conclusion: Critics keep the conversation honest, giving cinema the considered judgment and lively debate that good films deserve.
CineType.critic_strength_1_description: They can tell a good film from a great one and say why.
CineType.critic_strength_2_description: They turn a gut reaction into a clear, sharp case.
CineType.critic_strength_3_description: They love debating films as much as watching them.
CineType.critic_strength_4_description: Their verdicts are honest enough to actually rely on.
CineType.critic_strength_5_description: They know the canon and the standards it set.
CineType.critic_strength_6_description: They watch actively, weighing every choice a film makes.
CineType.critic_weakness_1_description: The urge to rate can override the joy of watching.
CineType.critic_weakness_2_description: They can dig in on a verdict past the point of reason.
CineType.critic_weakness_3_description: They can pick a film apart instead of letting it breathe.
CineType.critic_weakness_4_description: They can lean on the canon's standards over their own eyes.
CineType.critic_weakness_5_description: A friendly chat can turn into a debate they need to win.
CineType.critic_weakness_6_description: They can hold a film at arm's length to keep judging it.
CineType.programmer_tagline: Builds the festival lineup in their head.
CineType.programmer_epigraph: Once you overcome the one-inch-tall barrier of subtitles, you'll be introduced to so many more amazing films.
CineType.programmer_introduction: Programmers (CRMF) curate the future of cinema. Social and forward-looking, they seek out the bold and the new with a critic's discernment, then design the perfect way to share it with an audience.
CineType.programmer_how_they_watch: At festivals, in retrospectives, across themed double bills they've assembled themselves — always thinking about how films speak to each other and to a crowd.
CineType.programmer_what_they_love: Cutting-edge and world cinema, new auteurs, daring formal work — the films that should be seen. They love discovery and the craft of putting it in front of the right people.
CineType.programmer_blind_spots: Curating for impact, they can favor the important over the simply enjoyable, and chase the cutting edge past the point of pleasure.
CineType.programmer_with_others: They're a one-person film society — the friend who plans the double feature, writes the intro, and turns a watch into an event with a point of view.
CineType.programmer_growth: They grow by watching a film with no agenda and no audience in mind — just for themselves, with nothing to program.
CineType.programmer_conclusion: Programmers shape what cinema becomes, championing the new and the daring and making sure it finds the audience it deserves.
CineType.programmer_strength_1_description: They see how films connect and what audiences should see next.
CineType.programmer_strength_2_description: They turn a pile of films into a lineup with a point of view.
CineType.programmer_strength_3_description: They pair a sharp critical eye with an appetite for the new.
CineType.programmer_strength_4_description: They program for an audience and love the shared discovery.
CineType.programmer_strength_5_description: Bold, unproven, world cinema is exactly their territory.
CineType.programmer_strength_6_description: They can frame why a film matters and why now.
CineType.programmer_weakness_1_description: They can favor the important over the genuinely enjoyable.
CineType.programmer_weakness_2_description: They're always onto the next discovery, rarely sitting still.
CineType.programmer_weakness_3_description: Their cutting-edge taste can leave casual viewers behind.
CineType.programmer_weakness_4_description: Curating everything can squeeze out simple fun.
CineType.programmer_weakness_5_description: What's bold and new can matter more than what's good.
CineType.programmer_weakness_6_description: They can turn even a night off into a program.
